title: Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana
intro: Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza maculata and Dactylorhiza sambucina, and is also known as the spotted marsh orchid or the spotted orchid. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana include iridoid glycosides, such as 7-epi- genonin and 7-epi- genonin- 3-O-β- D-glucopyranoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana is a hybrid orchid species. It is a cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza maculata. As a hybrid, its classification is typically not included in the standard botanical nomenclature system. The hybrid status of Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana makes it challenging to classify in the traditional sense. It is often considered a taxonomic entity with a unique set of characteristics.

The specific types of flavonoids present in Dactylorhiza × koningweeniana include kaempferol and quercetin derivatives.
